name: Hunter.io API
what_it_does: Email finder and verification API that discovers professional email addresses by domain or name, verifies deliverability, checks for disposable or catch-all configurations, and returns confidence scores. Used for B2B sales prospecting and outreach automation.
use_cases: Finding professional email addresses for sales outreach agents given a company domain
Verifying email deliverability before sending automated campaigns
Discovering all publicly-known emails at a target domain for company mapping
Enriching CRM contact records with verified email addresses
Filtering out disposable and catch-all email addresses before list import
not_for: Phone number, LinkedIn, or social media enrichment
Jurisdictions where data brokering without consent is legally prohibited
best_when: An agent needs to find and verify professional email addresses for B2B outreach or CRM enrichment, and operates in regions where GDPR basis for processing is established.
avoid_when: You are targeting EU individuals without clear legitimate interest basis, need non-email contact data, or have a monthly volume exceeding plan search limits.

auth.notes: API key passed as query parameter (api_key=YOUR_API_KEY). No OAuth, no scopes. Single key grants full account access. Keep server-side only.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $49/mo (500 searches), Growth $149/mo (5,000 searches), Business $499/mo (50,000 searches)
pricing.notes: Free tier is suitable for prototyping only. Monthly search quota resets on billing date (not calendar month). Unused searches do not roll over on free plan. Paid plan pricing updated periodically - verify current pricing on site.

performance.rate_limits: No documented per-second limit; monthly quota enforced. Agents running bulk lookups should self-throttle to avoid hitting undocumented burst limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return a JSON body with an errors array, each containing id and details fields. Error codes are well-documented. HTTP 429 used for quota exhaustion. Agents can reliably parse error type from the id field.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only lookups and are naturally idempotent. Repeated calls with same parameters return same results without consuming additional quota from cache.
known_agent_gotchas: Monthly quota resets on billing date, not calendar month - quota tracking must account for this offset
Domain Search returns up to 100 results per page; use offset parameter for pagination on large companies
Email Finder returns status: 'found', 'guess', or 'notfound' - agents should handle 'guess' as low-confidence and threshold by score field
Email verification result accept_all: true means the server accepts all addresses - verification is unreliable for these domains
GDPR: finding and storing EU individual emails may require documented legitimate interest - Hunter provides data removal requests

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key passed as query parameter exposes key in server logs and URL history - prefer header-based auth where possible (Hunter does not support header auth). No scope granularity; single key grants full account access. No IP allowlist feature documented.
